idDocument.generic.description: Government-issued identity cards or permits other than passports; usually plastic cards or paper permits. Examples: driver's license/learner's permit, national ID card, residence/PR permit, military ID, social security card, state/territory photo ID, Aadhaar. Typical features: person's name, photo (SSN may lack), date of birth, ID/license/UIN number, issuing authority logo/seal, issue/expiry dates; may include address, signature, license class/restrictions; barcodes/QR codes or MRZ not starting with 'P<'. Excludes anything explicitly labeled 'Passport' or visa/stamp pages.

idDocument.passport.description: National passports (booklets) and passport cards. Strong cues: the word 'Passport' on cover or data page; data page with holder photo and fields like Passport No., Nationality, Date of Birth, Sex, Place of Birth, Date of Issue/Expiry, Authority, signature; two-line MRZ at bottom starting with 'P<'. Includes documents explicitly labeled 'Passport Card'. Excludes visas, entry/exit stamps, and covers shown alone without a visible data page.

mortgage.us.1003.description: Uniform Residential Loan Application (URLA). Borrower-completed form with sections like Borrower Information, Financial Information—Assets and Liabilities, Real Estate Owned, Declarations, Acknowledgments and Agreements, and Demographic Information (HMDA). Includes property/loan purpose, income, housing expenses, and loan originator details; signed by borrower(s). Often labeled Fannie Mae Form 1003/Freddie Mac Form 65. Distinct from underwriting summaries (1008), appraisal reports (1004), and employer verifications (1005).

mortgage.us.1004.description: Uniform Residential Appraisal Report (URAR). Appraiser-prepared valuation with sections: Subject, Contract, Neighborhood, Site, Improvements, Sales Comparison Approach with adjustment grid, Cost/Income approaches (as applicable), Comments, Appraiser's Certification and Limiting Conditions. Includes effective date of appraisal, opinion of value, photos (front/rear/street), map, sketch. Often references Fannie Mae 1004/Freddie Mac 70. Not an application (1003) or underwriting summary (1008).

mortgage.us.1005.description: Verification of Employment (VOE). Employer-completed form confirming borrower's employment status and income. Fields for employer name/address, position/title, start/end dates, base pay, overtime/bonus/commission (YTD and prior years), probability of continued employment; signed by employer/HR. May include a borrower authorization at top. Not an appraisal (1004) or loan application (1003).

mortgage.us.1008.description: Transmittal Summary (Underwriting summary). Lender/underwriter form summarizing loan and borrower risk: loan purpose, occupancy, property type, LTV/CLTV/HCLTV, DTI ratios, credit score, AUS findings, MI coverage, reserves, cash to close, income calculations, compensating factors, conditions. Often titled Transmittal Summary and referenced as Fannie Mae 1008/Freddie Mac 1077. Distinct from the borrower-signed 1003 and the appraisal 1004.

mortgage.us.closingDisclosure.description: TILA-RESPA Closing Disclosure (TRID). Five-page final disclosure with standardized sections: Loan Terms, Projected Payments, Costs at Closing, Closing Cost Details (Loan Costs/Other Costs), Calculating Cash to Close, Summaries of Transactions, Adjustments and Credits, Loan Disclosures, Contact Information, and Confirm Receipt. Shows borrower/seller amounts and cash to close. Distinct from the initial Loan Estimate (which should not be classified here).

CurrentPeriodGrossPay.description: Gross pay of the current period

invoice.description: Seller-issued request for payment. Typically shows “Invoice,” an invoice number, invoice date, Bill To/Ship To, line items with quantity, unit price, taxes, subtotal, terms (e.g., Net 30), due date, and balance due. May include bank/wire details. Exclude buyer-issued documents (purchase orders) and payment acknowledgments (receipts).

receipt.generic.description: Proof of payment for retail/restaurant or general POS transactions. Often shows “Receipt” or “Sales Receipt,” store name/location, date/time, itemized products or services (SKU/PLU), subtotal, tax, tip (optional), total paid, payment method (cash/card last 4), and change due. Exclude hotel folios (use receipt.hotel) and invoices requesting payment.

receipt.hotel.description: Hotel folio or lodging receipt acknowledging payment for a stay. Includes guest name, hotel name/address, room number/type, check-in/out dates, nightly rate, room charges, incidentals (parking, minibar), taxes/fees (occupancy/resort), and total paid/balance. May say “Folio,” “Guest Statement,” or “Hotel Receipt.” Exclude generic store/restaurant receipts.

purchaseOrder.description: Buyer-issued order to a vendor authorizing purchase. Typically labeled “Purchase Order” or “PO,” with PO number, buyer and supplier details, ship-to, requested delivery date, line items (qty, unit cost, part no.), and terms. Not a request for payment; exclude invoices and receipts.

utilityBill.description: Periodic bill from a utility provider (electricity, gas, water, sewer, internet/telecom). Shows service address, account number, billing period, usage details (e.g., kWh/therms/gal/GB), rate/charges breakdown, previous/last payment, amount due, and due date. May include meter readings and graphs. Exclude one-time sales invoices and generic receipts.

creditMemo.description: Billing adjustment note that changes the amount owed on a prior invoice. Includes credit memos/credit notes and debit memos. Cues: 'Credit Memo', 'Credit Note', or 'Debit Memo', reference to original invoice/PO, reason (return, price adjustment), negative or positive line items/amounts that reduce or increase balance, and revised balance/amount to be applied. Not a receipt (does not confirm payment) and not a standard invoice.

tax.us.1040.description: U.S. Individual Income Tax Return main form; shows filing status, dependents, income totals, adjustments, tax, credits, payments, refund/amount due. Title "Form 1040" at top; year prominently printed; signature lines for taxpayer and spouse.

tax.us.1040Schedule1.description: Schedule 1 (Additional Income and Adjustments to Income); reports items like business income (from Schedule C), unemployment, alimony received, and adjustments such as educator expenses, HSA deduction, student loan interest.

tax.us.1040Schedule2.description: Schedule 2 (Additional Taxes); reports alternative minimum tax, excess advance premium tax credit repayment, and other additional taxes that flow to Form 1040.

tax.us.1040Schedule3.description: Schedule 3 (Additional Credits and Payments); claims nonrefundable and refundable credits not listed on Form 1040 (e.g., foreign tax credit, education credits, net premium tax credit).

tax.us.1040Schedule8812.description: Schedule 8812 (Credits for Qualifying Children and Other Dependents); computes Child Tax Credit/Additional CTC; includes qualifying child tests and refundable credit calculations.

tax.us.1040ScheduleA.description: Schedule A (Itemized Deductions); details medical expenses, state/local taxes, mortgage interest, charitable gifts, casualty/theft losses; totals itemized deductions.

tax.us.1040ScheduleB.description: Schedule B (Interest and Ordinary Dividends); lists payers and amounts, totals interest/dividends, foreign account questions at Part III.

tax.us.1040ScheduleC.description: Schedule C (Profit or Loss From Business); sole proprietor business income/expenses; includes business name/EIN, Part I income, Part II expenses, cost of goods sold.

tax.us.1040ScheduleD.description: Schedule D (Capital Gains and Losses); summarizes short-term and long-term gains/losses from sales of capital assets; may reference Form 8949.

tax.us.1040ScheduleEIC.description: Schedule EIC (Earned Income Credit); lists qualifying children details (name, SSN, relationship, months lived with you) to claim EITC.

tax.us.1040ScheduleE.description: Schedule E (Supplemental Income and Loss); reports rental real estate, royalties, partnerships, S corporations, estates/trusts, REMICs; multiple properties/activities.

tax.us.1040ScheduleF.description: Schedule F (Profit or Loss From Farming); farm income/expenses, inventories, cost of livestock/feed, farm-specific categories.

tax.us.1040ScheduleH.description: Schedule H (Household Employment Taxes); household employer Social Security, Medicare, FUTA, and federal income tax withholding for household employees.

tax.us.1040ScheduleJ.description: Schedule J (Income Averaging for Farmers and Fishermen); computes tax using income averaging method across prior years.

tax.us.1040ScheduleR.description: Schedule R (Credit for the Elderly or the Disabled); calculates nonrefundable credit based on age/disability and income limits.

tax.us.1040ScheduleSE.description: Schedule SE (Self-Employment Tax); computes Social Security/Medicare tax on net self-employment income; includes optional methods and deduction for one-half SE tax.

tax.us.1040Senior.description: Form 1040-SR (U.S. Tax Return for Seniors); similar to 1040 but with larger print and a standard deduction chart for seniors

tax.us.1095A.description: Form 1095-A (Health Insurance Marketplace Statement); issued by Marketplace; lists monthly premiums, SLCSP, and advance premium tax credits for each covered individual.

tax.us.1095C.description: Form 1095-C (Employer-Provided Health Insurance Offer and Coverage); from large employer; includes Part II offer codes (Line 14), employee share (Line 15), and safe harbor codes (Line 16).

tax.us.1098.description: Form 1098 (Mortgage Interest Statement); reports mortgage interest, points, and mortgage insurance premiums; shows payer/borrower, property address, and boxes 1–11.

tax.us.1098E.description: Form 1098-E (Student Loan Interest Statement); reports student loan interest received by lender; includes borrower and lender info and interest amount.

tax.us.1098T.description: Form 1098-T (Tuition Statement); reports qualified tuition/related expenses, scholarships/grants, adjustments; includes student and school info.

tax.us.1099A.description: Form 1099-A (Acquisition or Abandonment of Secured Property); reports principal balance, fair market value, and dates for foreclosures/abandonments.

tax.us.1099B.description: Form 1099-B (Proceeds From Broker and Barter Exchange Transactions); details securities sales, cost basis, wash sales, term (short/long); often multi-page broker statements.

tax.us.1099CAP.description: Form 1099-CAP (Changes in Corporate Control and Capital Structure); reports cash/stock received and basis adjustments for shareholders in corporate actions.

tax.us.1099C.description: Form 1099-C (Cancellation of Debt); reports discharged debt amount, interest included, identifiable event code, and date of cancellation.

tax.us.1099Combo.description: Consolidated year-end brokerage tax statement combining multiple 1099 forms (e.g., 1099-DIV, 1099-INT, 1099-B, 1099-OID) in one packet; broker-branded with sections for each form.

tax.us.1099DA.description: Form 1099-DA (Digital Asset Proceeds from Broker Transactions) is issued by crypto/digital asset brokers and exchanges. It reports sales, exchanges, or other dispositions of digital assets such as cryptocurrency, stablecoins, and NFTs.The form includes broker/payer and recipient information, asset name/type, blockchain or contract details, wallet address, transaction ID/hash, quantity or units, dates acquired and disposed, gross proceeds, and—if reported—cost or other basis and adjustments.The header typically shows “Form 1099-DA” and references “Digital Asset” transactions. The layout often includes a tabular list of multiple transactions, similar to other 1099 forms.

tax.us.1099DIV.description: Form 1099-DIV (Dividends and Distributions); reports ordinary/qualified dividends, capital gain distributions, foreign tax paid, section 199A dividends.

tax.us.1099G.description: Form 1099-G (Certain Government Payments); reports unemployment compensation, state tax refunds, agricultural payments, or grants from government agencies.

tax.us.1099H.description: Form 1099-H (Health Coverage Tax Credit Advance Payments); reports monthly advance payments of the HCTC for eligible individuals.

tax.us.1099INT.description: Form 1099-INT (Interest Income); reports interest paid, early withdrawal penalty, U.S. savings bond interest, foreign tax paid.

tax.us.1099K.description: Form 1099-K (Payment Card and Third Party Network Transactions); reports gross payment transactions by month from payment processors (merchant acquiring/TPP).

tax.us.1099LS.description: Form 1099-LS (Reportable Life Insurance Sale); payor reports payment under life insurance policy sold or transferred; includes policy and seller info.

tax.us.1099LTC.description: Form 1099-LTC (Long-Term Care and Accelerated Death Benefits); reports long-term care benefits or accelerated death benefits; includes per diem vs. actual charges.

tax.us.1099MISC.description: Form 1099-MISC (Miscellaneous Information); reports rents, royalties, other income, prizes/awards, medical/legal payments; excludes nonemployee compensation (1099-NEC).

tax.us.1099NEC.description: Form 1099-NEC (Nonemployee Compensation); reports Box 1 nonemployee compensation to contractors; separate from 1099-MISC.

tax.us.1099OID.description: Form 1099-OID (Original Issue Discount); reports OID, acquisition premium, bond premium on debt instruments.

tax.us.1099PATR.description: Form 1099-PATR (Taxable Distributions Received From Cooperatives); reports patronage dividends and per-unit retain allocations.

tax.us.1099QA.description: Form 1099-QA (Distributions From ABLE Accounts); reports ABLE account distributions, earnings, and basis; identifies ABLE program.

tax.us.1099Q.description: Form 1099-Q (Payments From Qualified Education Programs); reports 529/Coverdell distributions, earnings, and basis amounts.

tax.us.1099R.description: Form 1099-R (Distributions From Pensions, Annuities, Retirement or Profit-Sharing Plans, IRAs, Insurance Contracts); includes distribution codes and taxable amount.

tax.us.1099SA.description: Form 1099-SA (Distributions From an HSA, Archer MSA, or Medicare Advantage MSA); reports gross distribution and earnings on excess contributions.

tax.us.1099SB.description: Form 1099-SB (Seller's Investment in Life Insurance Contract); issuer reports investment in the contract and surrender amount for transferred policies.

tax.us.1099S.description: Form 1099-S (Proceeds From Real Estate Transactions); reports gross proceeds from sale/exchange of real estate; shows property address/legal description.

tax.us.1099SSA.description: Form SSA-1099 (Social Security Benefit Statement); reports total Social Security benefits, Medicare premiums withheld, and repayments; SSA-issued.

tax.us.w2.description: Form W-2 (Wage and Tax Statement); shows wages, federal/state/local tax withheld, Social Security/Medicare wages and tips; multiple copies with employer EIN.

tax.us.w4.description: Form W-4 (Employee's Withholding Certificate); employee elections for federal tax withholding; includes filing status, multiple jobs, dependents, and adjustments.

other.description: Any document not matching the category above. Use other for partial excerpts or heavily redacted pages where the form type cannot be confidently identified.

segment.description: Divide the video into smaller segments by identifying changes in topic, scene, or idea—using visual or audio cues, dialogue shifts, or transitions as indicators. **IMPORTANT: If the video centers on a single main topic, segment it further by breaking out distinct subtopics. Avoid returning a single segment for the entire video.**

PartyAName.description: Legal name of the first party entering the agreement, typically the service provider or seller

TransactionDateTime.description: Date and time when the sales transaction occurred, used for receipts and point-of-sale records

fieldSchema.description:
1. Define your schema by specifying the fields you want to extract from the input files. Choose clear and simple `field names`. Use `field descriptions` to provide explanations, exceptions, rules of thumb, and other details to clarify the desired behavior.

2. For each field, indicate the `value type` of the desired output. Besides basic types like strings, dates, and numbers, you can define more complex structures such as `tables` (repeated items with subfields) and `fixed tables` (groups of fields with common subfields).

OrderDate.description: Date when the customer placed the order referenced by this invoice

DueDate.description: Date by which payment for the invoice is expected according to the agreed terms

PaymentTerms.description: Agreed terms specifying the period and conditions for payment of the invoice amount

CustomerNumber.description: Unique identifier assigned to the customer by the seller for internal tracking

CustomerVatNumber.description: Value Added Tax registration number of the customer for tax and regulatory purposes

CustomerOrderNumber.description: Reference number provided by the customer for their purchase order

SalesOrderNumber.description: Internal sales order number assigned by the seller to track the transaction

SalesDivision.description: Business division or unit of the seller responsible for the sale

ShipVia.description: Shipping method or carrier used to deliver the goods to the customer

InvoiceTo.description: Address and company details of the party being invoiced

ShipTo.description: Address and company details of the party receiving the shipment

RemitTo.description: Address to which payment should be sent for the invoice

WireTransferDetails.description: Banking information for wire transfer payments

Items.description: List of line items included in the invoice, each representing a product or service sold

items.description: Details of a single invoice line item

ItemNumber.description: Sequential number identifying the line item on the invoice

OrderQuantity.description: Quantity of the item ordered by the customer

ShipQuantity.description: Quantity of the item shipped to the customer

PartModelNumber.description: Manufacturer's part or model number for the item

UnitPrice.description: Price per unit of the item, excluding taxes and additional charges

UnitPriceCurrency.description: Currency code in which the unit price is denominated

Amount.description: Total amount for the line item, calculated as unit price times quantity

AmountCurrency.description: Currency code in which the amount is denominated

SubtotalAmount.description: Sum of all line item amounts before taxes and additional charges

SubtotalCurrency.description: Currency code in which the subtotal is denominated

VatTaxAmount.description: Value Added Tax or other applicable tax amount for the invoice

TotalAmount.description: Total amount due for the invoice, including all items and taxes

TotalCurrency.description: Currency code in which the total amount is denominated

OrderHandler.description: Name of the person responsible for handling the order within the seller's organization

CountryOfOrigin.description: Country where the goods originated or were manufactured

Transaction.description: Unique identifier of the Investment Transaction record. Return the raw ID string only (no label, no URL, no surrounding text).

OriginSystemUrl.description: Absolute https URL of the Investment Transaction record in an origin system such as Salesforce. Return only the URL; strip any trailing punctuation.

Name.description: Legal name of the portfolio company associated with this transaction. Return the entity name only (no labels, tickers, or aliases).

DateClosed.description: Close date in ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-dd. Return the date only.

Amount.description: Total invested amount in base currency units. Convert shorthand (e.g., 3.5M, 2bn) to the full number. Return digits with optional '.' decimal; no currency symbol, no thousands separators, no extra text.

AmountCurrency.description: ISO 4217 currency code (uppercase) for Amount. Infer from symbols or context when needed. Return the 3-letter code only; leave empty if ambiguous.

FAFund.description: Fund Accounting fund code allocating the shares for this transaction. Return the fund code only (no name or description).

FundGroup.description: Owning fund group for the shares in this transaction. Return the canonical group name or code only; normalize known variants.

PreMoney.description: Pre-money valuation amount in base currency units. Convert shorthand (e.g., 120M) to the full number. Return digits with optional '.' decimal only (no currency symbol or separators).

PreMoneyCurrency.description: ISO 4217 currency code (uppercase) for PreMoney. Infer when necessary. Return the code only; leave empty if ambiguous.

PostMoney.description: Post-money valuation amount in base currency units. Convert shorthand (e.g., 300M, 1.2B) to the full number. Return digits with optional '.' decimal only (no currency symbol or separators).

PostMoneyCurrency.description: ISO 4217 currency code (uppercase) for PostMoney. Infer when necessary. Return the code only; leave empty if ambiguous.

EstimatedOwnershipPercent.description: Estimated post-transaction ownership as a numeric value from 0 to 100. Return digits only (no % sign); e.g., '12.5%' -> 12.5.

Security.description: Purchased or issued security/instrument (e.g., Preferred Series A, Common, Convertible Note, SAFE). Normalize common variants where possible.

Notes.description: Single-line note relevant to this transaction. Return plain text only (no labels).

PortfolioCompanyCurrencyCode.description: ISO 4217 code for the portfolio company's functional currency. Order: AmountCurrency > ISO Currency Code in notes/terms > symbol in cap/PreMoneyCurrency/PostMoneyCurrency ('$'=USD only if no conflicts)>context (country/wire/cap table). May differ from transaction currency. Infer from symbols or context when needed. Return the 3-letter code only

value.description: Custom IRS 1040 form analyzer built with training files

fieldSchema.name: IRS_1040
fieldSchema.description:
1. Define your schema by specifying the fields you want to extract from the input files. Choose clear and simple `field names`. Use `field descriptions` to provide explanations, exceptions, rules of thumb, and other details to clarify the desired behavior.

2. For each field, indicate the `value type` of the desired output. Besides basic types like strings, dates, and numbers, you can define more complex structures such as `tables` (repeated items with subfields) and `fixed tables` (groups of fields with common subfields).

value.description: K-1 Form 1065 Schedule K-1 analyzer v3 - Using classify method for checkboxes to improve consistency

PageInfo.description: Header information from the top section of the K-1 form.

Tax_Year.description: The 4-digit tax year prominently displayed in the form header (e.g., 2024, 2025).

Tax_Begin_Date.description: The tax year beginning date, found near 'beginning' in the header.

Tax_End_Date.description: The tax year ending date, found near 'ending' in the header.

Part1_PartnershipInformation.description: Part I: Information About the Partnership (Boxes A-D). Top-left quadrant.

Issuer_ID.description: Box A: Partnership's EIN in format XX-XXXXXXX.

Issuer_Name.description: Box B: Partnership's legal business name.

IRS_Center.description: Box C: IRS Center city (e.g., 'OGDEN, UT').

Is_PTP.description: Box D: Is this a Publicly Traded Partnership? Look at the checkbox next to 'Check if this is a publicly traded partnership (PTP)'. If the checkbox has an X, checkmark, or is filled, classify as 'Yes'. If the checkbox is empty, classify as 'No'.

Part2_PartnerInformation.description: Part II: Information About the Partner (Boxes E-N). Bottom-left quadrant.

Recipient_ID.description: Box E: Partner's SSN (XXX-XX-XXXX) or TIN (XX-XXXXXXX).

Is_General_Partner.description: Box G: Is the partner a 'General partner or LLC member-manager'? Look at the checkbox. If checked (X or checkmark), classify as 'Yes'. If empty, classify as 'No'.

Is_Limited_Partner.description: Box G: Is the partner a 'Limited partner or other LLC member'? Look at the checkbox. If checked, classify as 'Yes'. If empty, classify as 'No'.

Is_Domestic_Partner.description: Box H1: Is this a 'Domestic partner'? Look at the checkbox. If checked, classify as 'Yes'. If empty, classify as 'No'.

Is_Foreign_Partner.description: Box H2: Is this a 'Foreign partner'? Look at the checkbox. If checked, classify as 'Yes'. If empty, classify as 'No'.

TableData.description: Boxes J, K, L: Tabular data for partner's share percentages, liabilities, and capital account. Extract ALL rows including: Profit, Loss, Capital (Box J), Nonrecourse, Qualified nonrecourse financing, Recourse (Box K), and all capital account rows (Box L).

Values.description: Values from the row, separated by pipe |.

K2_Includes_Lower_Tier_Liabilities.description: Box K2: Look for the checkbox next to text 'Check this box if item K1 includes liability amounts from lower-tier partnerships'. This checkbox is located AFTER the liability amounts section (Nonrecourse, Qualified nonrecourse, Recourse). If the checkbox has an X mark, checkmark, or any filled mark inside, classify as 'Checked'. If the checkbox is completely empty with no marks, classify as 'Unchecked'.

K3_Liabilities_Subject_To_Guarantees.description: Box K3: Look for the checkbox next to text containing 'Check if any of the above liability is subject to guarantees or other payment obligations by the partner'. This checkbox appears on the same line or immediately after the K2 checkbox text. IMPORTANT: Carefully examine this specific checkbox only - it is a small square box. If this checkbox has ANY mark inside it (X, checkmark, filled dot, or any other mark), classify as 'Checked'. If the checkbox is a completely empty square with no marks inside, classify as 'Unchecked'. Do not confuse with the K2 checkbox above it.

M_Partner_Contributed_Built_In_Gain_Loss.description: Box M: Look for text 'Did the partner contribute property with a built-in gain (loss)?' followed by 'Yes' and 'No' options with checkboxes. If the 'Yes' checkbox is checked, classify as 'Yes'. If the 'No' checkbox is checked or if neither is clearly checked, classify as 'No'.

Part3_LeftColumn.description: Part III Boxes 1-13: Income, deductions, credits. Right side of form, top half.

LineItems.description: All values in the box. For boxes like 9a, extract BOTH the main amount AND any letter codes (like 'A' for 28% rate gain). Do not stop at first value - extract all visible line items within box boundaries.

Code.description: Letter code if present (A, B, C, *, STMT).

Amount.description: Dollar amount or 'STMT'. Stay within box boundaries.

Part3_RightColumn.description: Part III Boxes 14-21: Credits, foreign items, AMT, distributions. Right side, bottom half.

LineItems.description: All values in the box.

Amount.description: Dollar amount or 'STMT'.

VendorName.description: Legal or business name of the company issuing the invoice, used for identification and payment purposes

VendorAddress.description: Mailing address of the vendor, used for correspondence and record keeping

CustomerName.description: Name of the customer or receiving company responsible for payment of the invoice

CustomerId.description: Unique identifier assigned to the customer for tracking and reference in billing systems

ReceivingCompany.description: Name of the company receiving the goods or services, used for delivery and record purposes

ReceivingCompanyAddress.description: Address of the receiving company, used for delivery of goods or services

ShippingAddress.description: Address where goods or services are shipped, used for delivery and logistics

RemittanceAddress.description: Address where payments should be sent, used for accounts receivable and payment processing

BillingAddress.description: Address where the invoice is billed, used for accounts payable and record keeping

ServiceAddress.description: Address where services are provided, used for operational and billing purposes

InvoiceId.description: Unique identifier assigned to the invoice for tracking and reference

InvoiceDate.description: Date when the invoice was issued, used for payment terms and record keeping

DueDate.description: Date by which payment for the invoice is due, used to determine payment deadlines

PaymentTerm.description: Terms specifying the period within which payment should be made, used for financial planning

value.description: MCP Analyzer for processing medical insurance claims. Segments input into claim form, medical report, and receipts. Analyzes each segment with appropriate analyzers. Returns the extracted fields from the claim form and key information from medical reports and receipts.

receipt.description: Receipts for medical expenses incurred as part of the claim. Can include pharmacy receipts, hospital bills, etc.

value.description: Analyzer for medical reports to extract key patient, treatment information and associated costs from discharge summaries and clinical notes. Translate all fields to English if the input document is in a different language.

MedicalFacility.description: Name of the clinic or hospital

DateAdmitted.description: Date the patient was first seen

DateOfDischarge.description: Date the patient was discharged if admitted

Description.description: The test ordered

Amount.description: total cost of the treatment

TotalCost.description: The total of all lab tests, hospital charges and fees

Currency.description: The currency that the costs are billed in

Receipt.description: Thermal printed receipt that uses come in top to down reading order used in restaurant, retail and consumer market

Other.description: Neither invoice nor receipt

LenderLoanNumber.description: Lender loan number or universal loan identifier

items.description: Extracted borrower information

Name.description: Borrower's full name as written on the form

CoBorrowerNames.description: Co-borrower's full name(s) as written on the form

IsNotApplicable.description: Indicates if current employment section does not apply

enumDescriptions.unsigned: Signature line is present but not signed.
enumDescriptions.notFound: No signature line or signature detected.

enumDescriptions.unsigned: Signature line is present but not signed.
enumDescriptions.notFound: No signature line or signature detected.

Amount.description: Loan amount

OtherRefinanceProgram.description: Other loan refinance program type (if provided)

StatementDate.description: Date the escrow review statement was issued to the borrower, indicating the effective date of the analysis

PropertyAddress.description: Physical address of the property associated with the mortgage and escrow account under review

LoanNumber.description: Unique identifier assigned to the mortgage loan for tracking and servicing purposes

CustomerServicePhone.description: Primary customer service contact number for inquiries related to the escrow account or mortgage

ShortageAmount.description: Total amount by which the escrow account falls below the required minimum balance, to be paid by the borrower

NewMonthlyMortgagePaymentAmount.description: Total monthly mortgage payment amount effective from the specified date, including principal, interest, escrow, and shortage payments

ShortageMonthlyPaymentAmount.description: Monthly payment amount added to cover the escrow shortage, typically spread over 12 months

EscrowPaymentStartDate.description: Date when the new monthly mortgage payment amount, including escrow adjustments, becomes effective

PrincipalAndInterestCurrent.description: Current monthly payment amount allocated to principal and interest before any changes

PrincipalAndInterestNew.description: New monthly payment amount allocated to principal and interest after escrow review

EscrowPaymentCurrent.description: Current monthly payment amount allocated to escrow for taxes and insurance

EscrowPaymentNew.description: New monthly payment amount allocated to escrow for taxes and insurance after review

TotalMonthlyMortgagePaymentCurrent.description: Current total monthly mortgage payment amount before any changes due to escrow review

TotalMonthlyMortgagePaymentNew.description: New total monthly mortgage payment amount after changes from escrow review

LowestProjectedEscrowBalance.description: Lowest projected balance in the escrow account during the upcoming year, used to determine shortage or surplus

MinimumRequiredEscrowBalance.description: Minimum balance required to be maintained in the escrow account, typically calculated as a multiple of the monthly escrow payment

AmountNeededToBringCurrent.description: Amount required to bring the escrow account up to the minimum required balance

EscrowAccountTrends.description: Summary of actual escrow disbursements and totals for the previous review period

PropertyTaxesActual.description: Total property taxes paid from escrow during the actual review period

TotalTaxesAndInsuranceActual.description: Total amount of taxes and insurance paid from escrow during the actual review period

MortgageInsuranceActual.description: Actual amount of mortgage insurance paid from escrow during the review period, if any

TotalActualEscrow.description: Total actual escrow disbursements for the review period

EscrowAccountProjections.description: Projected escrow disbursements and monthly payment calculations for the upcoming review period

PropertyTaxesProjected.description: Projected total property taxes to be paid from escrow for the upcoming year

PropertyInsuranceProjected.description: Projected total property insurance to be paid from escrow for the upcoming year

TotalTaxesAndInsuranceProjected.description: Projected total amount of taxes and insurance to be paid from escrow for the upcoming year

EscrowShortageProjected.description: Projected escrow shortage amount for the upcoming year

NewTotalMonthlyEscrowAmount.description: New total monthly escrow payment amount including projected shortage

EscrowAccountProjectionsTable.description: Month-by-month projection of escrow account activity for the upcoming year, including deposits, payments, and balances

items.description: Monthly escrow account projection entry

Date.description: Month and year for the projection entry

DepositAmount.description: Projected deposit to escrow for the month

PaymentAmount.description: Projected payment from escrow for the month

Description.description: Description of the payment or deposit, such as tax or insurance type

ProjectedEscrowBalance.description: Projected escrow account balance after the month's activity

fieldSchema.name: mySampleAnalyzer
fieldSchema.description:
1. Define your schema by specifying the fields you want to extract from the input files. Choose clear and simple `field names`. Use `field descriptions` to provide explanations, exceptions, rules of thumb, and other details to clarify the desired behavior.

2. For each field, indicate the `value type` of the desired output. Besides basic types like strings, dates, and numbers, you can define more complex structures such as `tables` (repeated items with subfields) and `fixed tables` (groups of fields with common subfields).

description.description: Describe what happened in the video segment. Include all significant details and be specific where possible. For example, use 'man,' 'woman,' 'child,' etc., rather than 'person,' and specify animals like 'dog' or 'cat.' For recognizable movie or animation characters, use their names if known.
examples: A close-up of a brown, leaf-like insect camouflaged against a green background.

audio_description.description: Generate a first-person audio description that narrates changes across scenes. Use 'we' and omit the movie title. Mention popular actors if known, and only include information that differs from the previous scene.
examples: We see a man adjusting his hat in a mirror, looking straight at us.

ungrounded_1.description: What is the main character's background or history? Analyze the character's actions and appearance to infer their background or history.
examples: The video shows a character involved in various activities, but their background is not mentioned.

ungrounded_2.description: What city was this video filmed in? Determine the city where the video was filmed based on visual cues.
examples: The video shows various locations but does not provide any explicit information about the city.

ungrounded_3.description: What are the character's intentions? Infer the character's intentions based on their actions and expressions.
examples: The video shows a character acting suspiciously, but their intentions are not clear.

ungrounded_4.description: Is the person in the video telling the truth? Evaluate the credibility of the character's statements.
examples: A character in the video makes a claim, but there is no evidence provided to verify it.

ungrounded_5.description: What is the relationship between the characters? Identify and describe the relationship between the characters based on their interactions.
examples: The video shows interactions between two people, but their relationship is not specified.

ungrounded_6.description: What time period is the video set in? Infer the time period of the video based on visual and contextual clues.
examples: The video contains scenes with various props and costumes, but the time period is not mentioned.

ungrounded_7.description: What is the underlying theme of the video? Analyze the events and visuals to determine the underlying theme.
examples: The video presents a series of events without explicitly stating the theme.

ungrounded_8.description: What are the character's motivations? Determine the motivations behind the character's actions.
examples: The video shows a character pursuing certain actions, but their motivations are not clear.

TaxYear.description: The tax year for this K-1 form. Look for 'For calendar year' or '20XX' near the top of the document. Format: YYYY (e.g., 2023, 2024).

PartnershipName.description: Partnership's name. Usually found in Part I section labeled 'Partnership's employer identification number', 'Partnership's name, address', or similar. Extract the full legal name of the partnership.

PartnershipEIN.description: Partnership's Employer Identification Number (EIN). Format: XX-XXXXXXX (9 digits with hyphen). Look in Part I section, typically labeled 'Partnership's employer identification number' or 'EIN'.

PartnerName.description: Partner's name. Found in Part II section, typically labeled 'Partner's name' or 'Name of partner'. Extract the full name as shown.

PartnerTIN.description: Partner's identifying number (SSN or EIN). Format: XXX-XX-XXXX or XX-XXXXXXX. Look in Part II section for 'Partner's identifying number', 'SSN', or 'EIN'.

OrdinaryBusinessIncome.description: Ordinary business income (loss) from Part III, Line 1. Look for 'Ordinary business income (loss)' or line labeled '1' in the income section. Extract the numerical amount, can be positive or negative.

NetRentalRealEstateIncome.description: Net rental real estate income (loss) from Part III, Line 2. Look for 'Net rental real estate income (loss)' or line labeled '2'. Extract numerical amount.

OtherNetRentalIncome.description: Other net rental income (loss) from Part III, Line 3. Look for 'Other net rental income (loss)' or line labeled '3'. Extract numerical amount.

GuaranteedPayments.description: Guaranteed payments for services or capital from Part III, Line 4. Look for 'Guaranteed payments' or line labeled '4'. Extract numerical amount.

InterestIncome.description: Interest income from Part III, Line 5. Look for 'Interest income' or line labeled '5'. Extract numerical amount.

DividendIncome.description: Dividend income from Part III, Line 6. Look for 'Dividend income', 'Dividends', or line labeled '6'. Extract numerical amount.

RoyaltyIncome.description: Royalties from Part III, Line 7. Look for 'Royalties' or line labeled '7'. Extract numerical amount.

NetShortTermCapitalGain.description: Net short-term capital gain (loss) from Part III, Line 8. Look for 'Net short-term capital gain (loss)' or line labeled '8'. Extract numerical amount.

NetLongTermCapitalGain.description: Net long-term capital gain (loss) from Part III, Line 9. Look for 'Net long-term capital gain (loss)' or line labeled '9'. Extract numerical amount.

FinalK1.description: Whether this is a Final K-1. Look for checkbox or text near 'Final K-1' at the top of the form. Return true if marked or indicated, false otherwise.

AmendedK1.description: Whether this is an Amended K-1. Look for checkbox or text near 'Amended K-1' at the top of the form. Return true if marked or indicated, false otherwise.

description.description: Describe what happened in the video segment. Include all significant details and be specific where possible. For example, use 'man,' 'woman,' 'child,' etc., rather than 'person,' and specify animals like 'dog' or 'cat.' For recognizable movie or animation characters, use their names if known.
examples: A close-up of a brown, leaf-like insect camouflaged against a green background.

audio_description.description: Generate a first-person audio description that narrates changes across scenes. Use 'we' and omit the movie title. Mention popular actors if known, and only include information that differs from the previous scene.
examples: We see a man adjusting his hat in a mirror, looking straight at us.

harm_1.description: Describe interactions between people in the video, analyzing and identifying any hate speech or discriminatory language.
examples: In a heated discussion, one person uses a racial slur against another.

harm_2.description: Identify and describe any violent scenes or physical altercations depicted in the video.
examples: A fight erupts between two groups, resulting in injuries.

harm_3.description: Identify and describe any scenes in the video that contain sexual content or solicitation.
examples: One person makes inappropriate advances towards another, suggesting a quid-pro-quo arrangement.

harm_4.description: Identify and describe any scenes in the video that depict or reference child exploitation.
examples: An adult directs a child to engage in illicit activities.

harm_5.description: Identify and describe any explicit sexual content depicted in the video.
examples: Scenes contain nudity and sexual interaction between adults.

harm_6.description: Identify and describe any biased or unfair treatment of individuals in the video based on characteristics such as race or gender.
examples: A person is excluded from the group because of their gender.

harm_7.description: Identify and describe any use of stereotypes in the video.
examples: The video presents certain cultural groups negatively, reinforcing harmful stereotypes.

harm_8.description: Identify and describe any depictions or discussions of self-harm or suicidal behavior in the video.
examples: A person shares struggles with self-harm, showing scars.

harm_9.description: Identify and describe any violent or graphic scenes in the video.

harm_10.description: Identify and describe any use of explicit or offensive language in the video.

harm_11.description: Identify and describe any content that promotes or glorifies terrorism in the video.
examples: A speaker praises a recent attack and encourages similar actions.

harm_12.description: Identify and describe any discriminatory behavior or language in the video.

harm_13.description: Identify and describe any scenes in the video that involve planning or discussing illegal activities.

harm_14.description: Identify and describe any instances of cyberbullying or harassment depicted in the video.

harm_15.description: Identify and describe any misinformation or disinformation presented in the video.

harm_16.description: Identify and describe any content in the video that invades someone's personal privacy.

harm_17.description: Identify and describe any instances of harassment or abuse in the video.
examples: One person verbally abuses and threatens another.

harm_18.description: Identify and describe any promotion of extremist ideologies in the video.

description.description: Describe what happened in the video segment. Include all significant details and be specific where possible. For example, use 'man,' 'woman,' 'child,' etc., rather than 'person,' and specify animals like 'dog' or 'cat.' For recognizable movie or animation characters, use their names if known.
examples: A close-up of a brown, leaf-like insect camouflaged against a green background.

audio_description.description: Generate a first-person audio description that narrates changes across scenes. Use 'we' and omit the movie title. Mention popular actors if known, and only include information that differs from the previous scene.
examples: We see a man adjusting his hat in a mirror, looking straight at us.

ungrounded_1.description: What is the main character's background or history? Analyze the character's actions and appearance to infer their background or history.
examples: The video shows a character involved in various activities, but their background is not mentioned.

ungrounded_2.description: What city was this video filmed in? Determine the city where the video was filmed based on visual cues.
examples: The video shows various locations but does not provide any explicit information about the city.

ungrounded_3.description: What are the character's intentions? Infer the character's intentions based on their actions and expressions.
examples: The video shows a character acting suspiciously, but their intentions are not clear.

ungrounded_4.description: Is the person in the video telling the truth? Evaluate the credibility of the character's statements.
examples: A character in the video makes a claim, but there is no evidence provided to verify it.

ungrounded_5.description: What is the relationship between the characters? Identify and describe the relationship between the characters based on their interactions.
examples: The video shows interactions between two people, but their relationship is not specified.

ungrounded_6.description: What time period is the video set in? Infer the time period of the video based on visual and contextual clues.
examples: The video contains scenes with various props and costumes, but the time period is not mentioned.

ungrounded_7.description: What is the underlying theme of the video? Analyze the events and visuals to determine the underlying theme.
examples: The video presents a series of events without explicitly stating the theme.

ungrounded_8.description: What are the character's motivations? Determine the motivations behind the character's actions.
examples: The video shows a character pursuing certain actions, but their motivations are not clear.

Summary.description: A one-paragraph description of the document.

DocumentType.description: Indicates the type of business document, such as agreement, invoice, or sales receipt, to classify the transaction or contract

EffectiveDate.description: The date on which the agreement or contract becomes effective and binding for all parties

PartyAName.description: Legal name of the first party entering the agreement, typically the service provider or seller

PartyAAddress.description: Principal business address of the first party in the agreement, used for legal and correspondence purposes

PartyBName.description: Legal name of the second party entering the agreement, typically the client or buyer

PartyBAddress.description: Principal business address of the second party in the agreement, used for legal and correspondence purposes

AccessFeeAmount.description: Monetary fee per access charged to the client for usage of software or bandwidth as specified in the agreement

AccessFeeThreshold.description: Maximum number of accesses at the standard fee rate before a reduced rate applies

ReducedAccessFeeAmount.description: Monetary fee per access charged after the threshold is exceeded, representing a discounted rate

PaymentDueDay.description: Day of the week by which payment for services rendered must be sent by the client to the provider

ServicePeriod.description: Range of dates during which services are provided or billed, typically used in invoices

InvoiceNumber.description: Unique identifier assigned to the invoice for tracking and reference purposes

InvoiceDate.description: Date on which the invoice is issued to the customer

InvoiceDueDate.description: Date by which payment for the invoice is required from the customer

CustomerName.description: Name of the customer or client to whom the invoice or agreement is addressed

CustomerId.description: Unique identifier assigned to the customer for internal tracking and billing purposes

BillToAddress.description: Address where the invoice is sent for payment processing, typically the finance department of the customer

ShipToAddress.description: Address where goods or services are delivered, as specified in the invoice or agreement

ServiceAddress.description: Location where services are performed or provided, as indicated in the invoice or contract

PurchaseOrderNumber.description: Reference number for the purchase order associated with the invoice or transaction

RemitToAddress.description: Address to which payment should be sent, typically the billing department of the provider

SalesAssociateName.description: Name of the sales associate or representative responsible for the transaction or sale

TransactionDateTime.description: Date and time when the sales transaction occurred, used for receipts and point-of-sale records

Items.description: List of items or services provided, each with details such as date, code, description, quantity, unit, price, tax, and amount

items.description: Details of a single item or service in the transaction

ItemDate.description: Date when the item or service was provided or billed

ItemCode.description: Unique code or identifier for the item or service

ItemDescription.description: Description of the item or service provided

ItemQuantity.description: Number of units or quantity of the item or service provided

ItemUnit.description: Unit of measure for the item or service, such as hours or pages

ItemPrice.description: Unit price for the item or service before tax

ItemTax.description: Tax amount applied to the item or service

ItemAmount.description: Total amount for the item or service including tax

SubtotalAmount.description: Sum of all item amounts before tax and additional charges, representing the base transaction value

SalesTaxAmount.description: Total sales tax applied to the transaction or invoice

TotalAmount.description: Total amount due for the transaction or invoice, including all items, taxes, and fees

PreviousUnpaidBalance.description: Outstanding balance from prior invoices or transactions that remains unpaid

AmountDue.description: Total amount currently due for payment, including new charges and any previous unpaid balance

AgreementSignatories.description: List of individuals who have signed the agreement, including their names and titles

SignatoryName.description: Name of the individual signing the agreement

SignatoryTitle.description: Title or position of the signatory within their organization

Diagnosis.description: Medical diagnosis determined during the hospital stay and documented on the discharge summary

Recommendations.description: Medical advice and instructions provided to the patient upon discharge, including dietary and follow-up guidance

ResponsiblePhysician.description: Name of the physician responsible for the patient's care and discharge documentation

CompanyLegalForm.description: Legal structure of the company as registered, indicating its corporate type and regulatory obligations

CompanyActivityCode.description: Primary business activity code (ΚΑΔ) assigned to the company for classification of its economic activities

CompanyTaxOffice.description: Name of the tax office (ΔΟΥ) responsible for the company's fiscal administration

CompanySizeClassification.description: Classification of company size according to official SME declaration, used for eligibility and reporting

EmployeeCount.description: Number of employees expressed in full-time equivalent units (ΕΜΕ) as declared in the SME statement

LastFiscalYearTurnoverAmount.description: Total turnover (sales revenue) of the company for the most recently closed fiscal year

CompanyOwners.description: List of individuals or entities holding at least 5% ownership in the company, including their details and participation percentage

OwnerSurname.description: Surname of the owner or shareholder

OwnerName.description: Given name of the owner or shareholder

OwnerTaxId.description: Tax identification number (ΑΦΜ) of the owner or shareholder

OwnershipPercentage.description: Percentage of company shares or participation held by the owner

CompanyAddress.description: Registered address of the company headquarters, including street, number, postal code, region, and other location details

CompanyContact.description: Primary contact information for the company, including phone, fax, and email

BusinessActivitySummary.description: Concise description of the company's main business activities, markets served, and operational scope

BusinessExpansionDescription.description: General description of the proposed investment or expansion activities, including objectives and target markets

WorkingCapitalPurpose.description: Purpose for which working capital is sought, specifying the intended use of funds in the context of the investment

InvestmentExpectedResults.description: Forecasted financial results for the company in the years following the investment, including turnover, EBITDA, net profit, and profit margin

ApiVersion.description: The version identifier of the Azure AI Content Understanding API used for processing and feature availability

ReleaseDate.description: The official release date of the Azure AI Content Understanding update or feature set

IsGeneralAvailability.description: Indicates whether the Azure AI Content Understanding service is in general availability status for production use
examples: true

ModelSelectionType.description: Specifies the deployment type for Foundry models, such as Global, DataZone, or Regional, to optimize for quality, latency, and cost
examples: Global

ProvisionedThroughputUnits.description: Number of reserved capacity units for model deployment to support predictable, high-volume workloads

PricingModel.description: Describes the pricing structure for content extraction, contextualization, and generative model usage, including token-based charges
examples: Transparent pricing model

InputRangeSupport.description: Indicates if the service supports analyzing specific input ranges or sections of a document to reduce token consumption
examples: true

TableOutputFormat.description: Specifies the supported output formats for tables, such as HTML or Markdown, to match downstream processing requirements
examples: Markdown

RegionCount.description: Number of geographic regions where Azure AI Content Understanding is available

StudioAvailability.description: Indicates if Azure AI Content Understanding is available in Content Understanding Studio for interactive use
examples: true

RestApiAvailability.description: Indicates if Azure AI Content Understanding is available through the REST API for programmatic access
examples: true

FieldCountLimit.description: Maximum number of fields supported for extraction across all modalities in the service

TokensObjectSupport.description: Indicates if the API response includes input, output, and contextualization tokens consumed as part of the tokens object
examples: true

InvoiceNumber.description: Unique identifier assigned to the tax invoice for tracking and reference purposes

InvoiceDate.description: Date on which the invoice was issued to the recipient

DueDate.description: Date by which payment for the invoice is expected from the recipient

TotalDueAmount.description: Total amount payable by the recipient as indicated on the invoice, including taxes and adjustments

SubtotalAmount.description: Sum of all item amounts before taxes and other adjustments

IGSTAmount.description: Total Integrated Goods and Services Tax charged on the invoice

SGSTAmount.description: Total State Goods and Services Tax charged on the invoice

CGSTAmount.description: Total Central Goods and Services Tax charged on the invoice

AdvanceAmount.description: Advance payment amount received prior to invoice issuance, if any

TotalAmount.description: Grand total amount including all taxes and adjustments as shown on the invoice

SupplierName.description: Legal name of the supplier issuing the invoice

SupplierAddress.description: Physical address of the supplier as printed on the invoice

SupplierGSTIN.description: Goods and Services Tax Identification Number of the supplier

SupplierPAN.description: Permanent Account Number of the supplier for tax purposes

RecipientName.description: Legal name of the recipient to whom the invoice is addressed

RecipientAddress.description: Physical address of the recipient as printed on the invoice

StateCode.description: Code representing the state where the supply is made, as per GST regulations

PlaceOfSupply.description: Location where the goods or services are supplied, as per GST rules

RecipientGSTIN.description: Goods and Services Tax Identification Number of the recipient

AccountDetails.description: Bank account information for payment to the supplier

InvoiceItems.description: List of goods or services provided, including description, tax rates, and amounts

items.description: Details of each invoiced item

Description.description: Description of the goods or services supplied

HSNSAC.description: Harmonized System of Nomenclature or Service Accounting Code for the item

IGSTRate.description: Integrated GST rate applicable to the item

SGSTRate.description: State GST rate applicable to the item

CGSTRate.description: Central GST rate applicable to the item

Amount.description: Amount charged for the item, excluding taxes

Boolean.description: Extract Final K-1 and Amended K-1 ONLY from the top section of the document. Look only in the header or the first 4 lines.  Do not use values from anywhere else in the document.

Tax_Year.description: Tax year stated on the K‑1 (often near the top header). Format: YYYY (e.g., 2024). If not determinable, return null.

Issuer.description: B Partnership's name, address, city, state and ZIP code

IRS_Center.description: IRS Center where partnership filled return

PTP.description: Check if this is publicly traded partnership (PTP)

Recipient.description: Name, address, city, state, and ZIP code

Partner_Type.description: Text content. Visual Cue: Checkboxes may span multiple lines; read all text associated with the checked and unchecked option. This includes Box G, H1, H2, I1, I2, sale or, exchange of partnership interest

Name.description: Name

Entity_Type.description: What type oof entity is this partner?

TableData.description: For boxes J, K, L which look like grids or tables (rows and columns). Extract the data structured as rows.

RowLabel.description: The leftmost text in the row (e.g., 'Beginning', 'Ending').

Values.description: The numbers/values in the columns to the right of the row label, separated by | between beginning and Ending

K2_K3_M.description: Text content. Visual Cue: Checkboxes may span multiple lines; read all text associated with the checked and unchecked option. This includes Box K2, K3, M

Part3_LeftColumn.description: Extract Boxes 1 through 13. Visual Location: The top half of the RIGHT column of the page.

BoxNumber.description: The bold number (1-13) in the top left corner of the box.

BoxDescription.description: The text label describing the box content.

LineItems.description: Look for one or more lines of values. Some boxes (like 11 or 13) have letter codes (A, B, C, *, STMT) followed by amounts or STMT

Code.description: The bold letter code (e.g. A, B, C) if present to the left of the amount.

Description.description: Description text if present next to the code.

Amount.description: The alphanumerical amount (e.g. STMT, * 500). DO NOT CROSS over vertical lines.

Part3_RightColumn.description: Extract Boxes 14 through 21. Visual Location: The bottom half of the RIGHT column of the page.

LineItems.description: Values within the box. Look specifically for multiple lines with different codes. Box 1 - 13 ONLY

Code.description: The letter code (e.g. A, B, C, *, AG*).

Description.description: The letter code (e.g. A, B, C, *, AG*).

Amount.description: The alphanumerical amount (e.g. STMT, * 500).

Middle_Part.description: Extract Boxes 22 through 23. Visual Location: The bottom half of the LEFT and RIGHT column of the page.

PageInfo.description: Header section: tax year, form status checkboxes.

Final_K_1_Amended_K_1.description: Header checkboxes near 'Final K-1' and 'Amended K-1' labels only. Do not extract checkboxes from other sections.

Tax_End_Date.description: Tax year ending date near 'ending' label.

Tax_Begin_Date.description: Tax year beginning date near 'beginning' label.

Part1_PartnershipInformation.description: Part I: Information About the Partnership (Boxes A-D).

Issuer.description: Box B: Partnership name and address.

IRS_Center.description: Box C: IRS Center where partnership filed.

PTP.description: Box D: Publicly traded partnership checkbox.

Part2_PartnerInformation.description: Part II: Information About the Partner (Boxes E-N).

Recipient_ID.description: Box E: Partner's SSN or TIN.

Recipient.description: Box F: Partner's name and address.

Partner_Type.description: Boxes G, H1, H2, I1, I2: Partner classification checkboxes.

String.description: Checkbox label (e.g., 'General partner or LLC member-manager', 'Limited partner or other LLC member', 'Domestic partner', 'Foreign partner').

Name.description: DE's name.

TableData.description: Boxes J, K, L, N tabular data. Extract ALL rows: Box J (Profit, Loss, Capital with Beginning/Ending %), Box K1 (Nonrecourse, Qualified nonrecourse financing, Recourse with Beginning/Ending $), Box L (Beginning capital account, Capital contributed during the year, Current year net income (loss), Other increase (decrease), Withdrawals and distributions, Ending capital account), and Box N (Partner's Share of Net Unrecognized Section 704(c) Gain or (Loss) with Beginning/Ending $). Include every row with numeric values.

RowLabel.description: Row label text. Include Box N row: 'Partner's Share of Net Unrecognized Section 704(c) Gain or (Loss)'.

Values.description: Numeric values. Use pipe '|' to separate multiple columns.

K2_K3_M.description: Three checkboxes: K2 (lower-tier partnership liabilities), K3 (liability guarantees), M (built-in gain/loss property). Extract exactly 3 items.

Part3_LeftColumn.description: Part III Boxes 1-13: Income and deductions.

LineItems.description: All line items in this box. Extract every code/amount pair.

Code.description: Letter code (A, B, *, STMT) if present.

Description.description: Line item description if present.

Amount.description: Dollar amount or 'STMT'.

Part3_RightColumn.description: Part III Boxes 14-21: Credits, foreign transactions, AMT, other info.

LineItems.description: All line items in this box.

Description.description: Line item description if present.

Amount.description: Dollar amount or 'STMT'.

Middle_Part.description: Boxes 22-23: Activity and K-3 attachment checkboxes.

AddendumTitle.description: Extract the full title of Addendum A including any section notation. Example: 'Addendum A List of Account Numbers'

TableSectionHeader.description: Extract the exact column header text for the account column on page 3, which reads 'ACCOUNT Section 1 :'. BY EXAMINING THE PAGE IMAGE CAREFULLY: The '1' after 'Section' is formatted as SUPERSCRIPT - it appears VISUALLY SMALLER and RAISED ABOVE THE BASELINE compared to the surrounding text 'Section'. Extract this header with special notation to indicate the superscript: use 'ACCOUNT Section 1^' or 'ACCOUNT Section ¹' to preserve the superscript indicator. Look at the vertical alignment and font size in the image to confirm the '1' is raised and smaller. This is critical for understanding document structure.

AccountList.description: Extract all accounts from Section 1 of the account table (the first section with payment terms on page 3). Use the page image to accurately read the table. Include the account number, business name/address, and payment terms. Extract up to 10 entries.

AccountNumber.description: The account number from the first column of the ACCOUNT Section 1 table on page 3. CRITICAL: Many account numbers in this table have SUPERSCRIPT markers after them (visually smaller and raised). BY EXAMINING THE PAGE IMAGE: Look at each account number carefully. Examples: '0071¹' (has superscript 1), '0141¹' (has superscript 1), '4211¹' (has superscript 1). Extract the FULL account number INCLUDING the superscript marker. Represent it as either '0071¹' or '0071^1'. The superscript is a critical part of the account identifier - do NOT omit it. Pay special attention to the vertical positioning and size of any character after the main number.

BusinessName.description: The primary business name from the name and address column (e.g., 'AAA', 'BBB', 'CCC')

FullAddress.description: The complete address including street, city, state, and ZIP code

PaymentTermsDays.description: The payment terms in days from the third column (e.g., '7', '04' or empty if not specified)

FootnoteMarkers.description: Extract any footnote markers or superscript indicators found in the document by examining the page images. Look especially at table headers and note whether text is visually raised or smaller.

Marker.description: The actual superscript or footnote marker (e.g., '1', '**', etc.)

Location.description: Where this marker appears (e.g., 'After Section heading', 'In Commodity Tier column header', etc.)

IsSuperscript.description: True if this appears to be in superscript format visually when examining the page image (smaller text, raised above baseline)

period_start_date.description: The start date of the pay period

period_end_date.description: The end date of the pay period

attendance_records.description: Array of attendance records for the employee

items.description: A single attendance record

date.description: The date of the attendance record

attendance_type.description: The type of attendance (e.g., Regular, Holiday, Sick)

regular_hours.description: The number of regular hours worked

overtime_hours.description: The number of overtime hours worked

SellerName.description: Legal or business name of the entity issuing the invoice and providing goods or services

SellerAddress.description: Mailing address of the seller or service provider as printed on the invoice

SellerMobile.description: Contact mobile phone number of the seller or service provider

SellerEmail.description: Contact email address of the seller or service provider

BuyerName.description: Legal or business name of the entity being billed for goods or services

BuyerAddress.description: Mailing address of the buyer or recipient of the invoice

InvoiceNumber.description: Unique identifier assigned to the invoice for tracking and reference purposes

InvoiceDate.description: Date on which the invoice was issued to the buyer

DueDate.description: Date by which payment for the invoice is expected from the buyer

InvoiceItems.description: List of goods or services provided, including description, quantity, rate, and amount for each line item

items.description: Details of a single invoice line item

SerialNumber.description: Sequential number identifying the line item within the invoice

Description.description: Brief description of the product or service billed in this line item

Quantity.description: Number of units or instances of the product or service billed in this line item

Rate.description: Unit price or rate charged for each unit of the product or service in this line item

Amount.description: Total price for the line item, calculated as quantity multiplied by rate

SubtotalAmount.description: Sum of all line item amounts before any additional charges, discounts, or taxes

PayeeName.description: Name of the individual or entity to whom payment should be made as specified in payment instructions

TotalAmount.description: Total amount due for the invoice including all charges and adjustments

PaidAmount.description: Amount already paid towards the invoice as of the specified payment date

PaidDate.description: Date on which payment was made towards the invoice

BalanceDueAmount.description: Remaining amount to be paid by the buyer after accounting for any payments already made

AuthorizedSignatory.description: Name or designation of the person who has authorized or signed the invoice

Summary.description: Each input line is a tuple of: field name for which some value was extracted, predicted normalized value, gt normalized value and lastly the reasons for which llm classified this as an error (could be either prediction or gt is missing, prediction may point to a different entity or at a wrong location - especially relevant for checkboxes ).  classify the differences between gt and prediction and create a summary table where each row is an object type (Boolean true/false, date, number, string, etc ) and each column is: the % of differences due to missing automated value and missing manual value, main reasons error (with approximate frequency and 1 full input line example for each reason) for which this is deemed an. Finally, produce a report of the trends that show what factors could have made either gt or prediction wrong. Return the answer in a nicely formatted html code.

IssuesToReview.description: List out all suspicious content in the document, including invalid content (wrong format, fake value), inconsistent values (some entities are shown up in different verbatim text), missing information (miss required signature, date, etc.).

Description.description: A formal statement why these reference clauses should be reviewed.

ReferenceClauses.description: List of paragraphes in the document which should be reviewed for the indicated issue type.

Parties.description: Can be a contractor, service provider or government office. Include parties mentioned in an appendix if applicable

Clause.description: A formal statement about a part, which contains information including address, formal name and reference names.

ReferenceNames.description: One party can have multiple reference names, as indicated in the document

Regions.description: One Jurisdiction clause can have multiple associated regions.

Duration.description: The duration mentioned in the document.

ExpirationDate.description: The date when the contract expires. Might not explicitly show up in the document; instead, it needs to be reasoned with effective date and duration.

invoice.description: Seller-issued request for payment. Typically shows “Invoice,” an invoice number, invoice date, Bill To/Ship To, line items with quantity, unit price, taxes, subtotal, terms (e.g., Net 30), due date, and balance due. May include bank/wire details. Exclude buyer-issued documents (purchase orders) and payment acknowledgments (receipts).

receipt.generic.description: Proof of payment for retail/restaurant or general POS transactions. Often shows “Receipt” or “Sales Receipt,” store name/location, date/time, itemized products or services (SKU/PLU), subtotal, tax, tip (optional), total paid, payment method (cash/card last 4), and change due. Exclude hotel folios (use receipt.hotel) and invoices requesting payment.

receipt.hotel.description: Hotel folio or lodging receipt acknowledging payment for a stay. Includes guest name, hotel name/address, room number/type, check-in/out dates, nightly rate, room charges, incidentals (parking, minibar), taxes/fees (occupancy/resort), and total paid/balance. May say “Folio,” “Guest Statement,” or “Hotel Receipt.” Exclude generic store/restaurant receipts.

other.description: Any document not matching the category above. Use other for partial excerpts or heavily redacted pages where the form type cannot be confidently identified.

SegmentType.description: The short title or a short summary of the story or chapter.

Description.description: A five-word description of the scene. A scene is a smaller segment of the segment where a continous block for storytelling unfolds within a specific time, place, and set of characters. A scene can only belong to a single chapter, and cannot overlap with other scenes. Scenes are sequential across the video.

StartTimestamp.description: the start timestamp of the scene

EndTimestamp.description: the end timestamp of the scene
